key_departures.impact: Posted 14.5 sacks over two seasons in Denver, serving as the engine of one of the NFL's top-5 defenses. Signed a 3-year, $63M deal with $42M guaranteed with Tennessee. His versatility as a pass rusher who could operate inside and outside made him nearly irreplaceable. Massive hole at IDL/EDGE-hybrid.

Released with post-June 1 designation on March 12, saving $8.19M cap. Greenlaw had an injury-riddled and suspended one-year tenure in Denver, playing just 8 games with 43 tackles. Was effectively a non-factor. Departure is neutral-to-positive — Singleton and Strnad are the clear starters. Returns to 49ers on a 1-year, $7.5M deal.

Six-year Broncos veteran and reliable depth/spot-starter safety departed on a 1-year, $5M deal with Dallas. Started 5 games in 2025 including late-season when Brandon Jones went down. Made a clutch interception of Josh Allen in the Divisional Round playoff win vs. Buffalo. His departure creates a depth hole at S behind Hufanga and Jones.

Broncos declined to tender McLaughlin as an RFA, allowing him to hit unrestricted free agency. Had 41 touches in 8 games in 2025 after Dobbins' injury, averaging 5.1 yards per carry. Depth loss at RB — not a need-creating departure given Dobbins re-signing and RJ Harvey on roster.

signings.contract_notes: Can scale to $20M with $2M/yr incentive at 1,200 scrimmage yards. Base value confirmed at $16M per NBC Sports. $8M fully guaranteed in Year 1 only. Year 2 non-guaranteed.
signings.analysis: Dobbins led Denver in carries (153), rushing yards (772), and 10+ yard runs (21) before a season-ending Lisfranc injury in Week 10. At $8M AAV (base), this is starter-tier money for a back with elite productivity when healthy. However, he hasn't played more than 13 games in a season since his rookie year in 2020, and only Year 1 is fully guaranteed. Denver missed out on top RB options (Kenneth Walker III to KC at $14M/yr; Travis Etienne to NO at $13M/yr). Dobbins re-signing partially addresses RB need but does not fully resolve it given his chronic injury history. The team will likely still add a RB via draft. This is a 1B alongside second-year back RJ Harvey, not a standalone solution.

signings.contract_notes: Structure: 2026 — $3M signing bonus + $3.99M base (fully gtd) + $510K per-game roster bonus. 2027 — $6.735M base ($3.5M fully gtd) + $765K per-game roster bonus. Confirmed by DNVR/ESPN.
signings.analysis: Singleton led the Broncos with 135 tackles in 2025 despite battling and overcoming testicular cancer mid-season. His re-signing ensures stability and leadership at ILB. $7.75M AAV is starter-tier for LB, and $11M guaranteed reflects genuine commitment. However, at 32, this is not a long-term solution — the Broncos will still want to draft LB talent. With Singleton and Strnad both back, LB drops from a need but remains a draft-day target for future succession planning. Partially addressed due to age and the simultaneous release of Greenlaw.

signings.contract_notes: Confirmed 3yr/$18M with $10M guaranteed per ESPN. Signed before legal tampering began.
signings.analysis: Strnad emerged as a valuable two-way linebacker and one of Denver's best special teamers. The 3-year commitment at $6M AAV is reasonable market value and signals genuine investment. Combined with the Singleton re-signing, Denver has their starting ILB duo locked in. The three-year structure on Strnad (who turns 30 in August) represents a low-risk, moderate-investment retention. Together, Strnad + Singleton partially address LB — starter need is met for 2026, but long-term succession remains a legitimate draft need.

signings.contract_notes: Can scale to $18.5M with incentives per Denver Post. $9.5M guaranteed confirmed by Spotrac/Denver Post.
signings.analysis: Trautman is Denver's most-deployed TE (57% snap share in 2025, 20 catches, 195 yds, 1 TD) and a premier run blocker. His reliability (56-of-56 games with the Broncos, no drops) is his primary value. At $5.67M AAV, this is below the starter market at TE. The Broncos still have Evan Engram (trap cut candidate), Nate Adkins, and Lucas Krull on the roster, but none are difference-makers in the passing game. The TE room is serviceable but not upgraded — Trautman retention is maintenance of the status quo, not an upgrade. TE remains a viable draft target.

signings.contract_notes: Signed for below the $3.52M lowest RFA tender amount per The Denver Post. Exact dollar amount not fully confirmed; estimated slightly below $3.52M. Figure listed is an estimate.
signings.analysis: H-back/flex TE retained below tender value. Primarily a blocking TE and special teamer. One-year deal signals no long-term commitment. Rotational contributor. Does not meaningfully change TE need picture.

signings.contract_notes: Re-signed on a one-year deal, terms near minimum. Part of the wave of depth re-signings on March 11.
signings.analysis: Krull suffered a season-ending Jones fracture in his foot in 2025, playing only 3 games. Retained to bring back entire TE room from 2025. Camp/depth body. No need impact.

signings.contract_notes: Base value $9.5M; can scale to $11M. $5.6M guaranteed per Denver Post/Mike Klis. Signed March 5, before tampering.
signings.analysis: Versatile OL swing man who started 10 games at LG in 2025 filling in for injured Ben Powers. Both Powers and the full 5-man starting line remain under contract for 2026, so Palczewski enters as the top reserve. Could ascend to a starting role if Ben Powers is traded (mentioned as a trade candidate). Two-year deal is modest and appropriate for depth/swing lineman. Does not affect IOL need picture.
Backup QB retained behind Bo Nix. Standard depth retention. No need impact. Note: Jarrett Stidham (QB3) is reported to be a trade target for other teams.

signings.previous_team: Denver Broncos (re-sign, via in-season return from Giants P-squad)
signings.contract_notes: Veteran minimum ~$1.3M confirmed by Denver Post/Denver Gazette. Officially announced March 12.
signings.analysis: Long-time Sean Payton favorite, a blocking WR with situational passing-game value (29-yard TD vs. Buffalo in Divisional Round). Slots in as WR5 behind Sutton, Troy Franklin, Marvin Mims, Pat Bryant. Veteran-minimum deal means zero cap cost and no commitment. Does nothing to address the need for a legitimate WR2 option opposite Courtland Sutton. Denver missed out on Romeo Doubs ($70M, Patriots) and did not pursue top WR targets. WR remains a significant draft-day need.

signings.contract_notes: Minimum deal per Denver Post (March 11 report). Exact figure near $1.075M ERFA level.
signings.analysis: Reserve DL who missed all of 2025 with injury. Career totals: 40 tackles, 1 sack in 34 career games. Could potentially compete for snaps vacated by Franklin-Myers departure but is not a remotely comparable replacement. IDL need escalated by Franklin-Myers' departure remains largely unaddressed.

signings.contract_notes: Fullback re-signed at near minimum per Denver Post.
signings.analysis: Fullback who took over after Michael Burton's injury in 2025. Started 3 games, showed surprising pass-catching ability on 17 touches. His return limits Adkins' H-back duties. Minimum deal, no cap relevance. No need impact.

signings.contract_notes: ERFA tender at $1.075M confirmed per Denver Gazette (signed March 7).
signings.analysis: Reserve RB entering his 5th season in Denver. Depth body. No meaningful impact on RB need.

Special teams standout and depth S. Tendered at minimum. With PJ Locke departed, Devon Key is now likely the primary backup option behind starters Hufanga and Jones. Does not solve the safety depth need created by Locke's departure but provides a familiar body.
Depth OLB/EDGE retained at minimum. No meaningful impact on IDL/pass-rush need.

signings.contract_notes: Second-round RFA tender at $5.81M for 2026. Confirmed by multiple sources.
signings.analysis: Young developmental CB retained via 2nd-round tender. Made a pivotal interception vs. Buffalo in the Divisional Round. CB is not a significant pre-FA need with Pat Surtain II locked up. This is a smart retention of an ascending player. No draft-need implications.

recommended_need_adjustments.reasoning: John Franklin-Myers (7.5 sacks in 2025, 14.5 over two seasons) departed to Tennessee on a 3yr/$63M deal. No meaningful replacement was signed. Henningsen ($1.075M, missed all of 2025 with injury) is a camp body, not a starter. The Broncos' pass rush was predicated on Franklin-Myers' inside/outside versatility. IDL/pass-rush should now be treated as the Broncos' single biggest draft need. Escalate to Tier 1.

P.J. Locke departed to Dallas on a 1-year, $5M deal. While Devon Key (ERFA tender, $1.075M) remains as depth, Denver now lacks a proven third safety. Hufanga and Jones are the starters, but Jones' health coming off injury is a question mark. Locke was a reliable spot-starter and key special teamer who also made a playoff-clinching interception vs. Buffalo. No meaningful outside free agent signed at S. Escalate to Tier 2.

Denver did not sign a single outside free agent at WR despite monitoring multiple targets (Romeo Doubs to NE at $70M; Kendrick Bourne). Humphrey's veteran-minimum re-sign is depth noise, not a solution. The WR2 role behind Courtland Sutton remains unfilled by a reliable starter-caliber player. Troy Franklin, Marvin Mims, and Pat Bryant are all still developmental/unproven. WR need unchanged at Tier 2 — viable round 1 or early round 2 draft option.

Re-signing both Alex Singleton (2yr/$15.5M, $11M gtd) and Justin Strnad (3yr/$18M, $10M gtd) keeps the 2025 starting ILB duo intact. Greenlaw's release was subtracted but he was a non-factor in 2025 (8 games, mostly injured). LB is now adequately staffed for 2026 starters. However, Singleton is 32 and on a 2-year deal — the Broncos will want a developmental LB drafted for long-term succession. Drop from Tier 2 to Tier 3.

Dobbins re-signing (2yr/$16M base, $8M gtd) retains the team's leading rusher who is elite when healthy. Paired with second-year back RJ Harvey, Denver has a legitimate starting backfield. However, Dobbins' injury history (hasn't played 14+ games since 2020) and the non-guaranteed Year 2 mean risk remains. Jaleel McLaughlin let go creates a depth question. Denver may still add a RB via draft (likely Rounds 3-5). Drop from Tier 2 to Tier 3 — addressed for now, but draft depth still prudent.

No change. The full TE room (Trautman 3yr/$17M, Adkins 1yr, Krull 1yr, Engram on existing contract) was retained. Trautman is serviceable as a blocking TE and reliable pass-catcher, but the room as a whole lacks a dangerous receiving threat. Evan Engram had the second-worst drop rate among TEs with 25+ targets. TE remains a moderate draft need in Rounds 2-4 for a pass-catcher upgrade. Tier unchanged.

No change. Ben Powers ($18.15M cap hit, no guarantees) is a potential trade/cut candidate but was retained for now. Palczewski re-signed as quality depth/swing lineman. Starting 5 remains intact. IOL is not a pressing need but could become one if Powers is moved. Tier unchanged.

key_additions.headline: Denver retains its 2025 leading rusher — elite when healthy but chronic injury history limits commitment to Year 1 only
Team captain and leading tackler (135 in 2025) re-signed as defensive anchor and locker room cornerstone
Starting ILB partner to Singleton locked in on longest deal of Denver's run-it-back offseason
Denver's most-deployed TE (57% snap share) re-signed as the reliable run-blocking foundation of Sean Payton's offense
Versatile swing lineman retained after proving himself as an adequate LG starter mid-season in 2025
Payton's trusted blocking WR back on a minimum deal after a playoff-game touchdown vs. Buffalo

key_losses.headline: Denver's best pass rusher and top free agent (14.5 sacks over 2 seasons) cashes in with Titans, leaving a massive IDL void
Six-year Broncos veteran safety departs after a clutch playoff run, thinning Denver's depth behind Hufanga and Jones
Disappointing one-year tenure ends with a post-June 1 cut; Greenlaw played just 8 games due to injuries and suspension
Speedy change-of-pace back let go without a tender after Dobbins re-signing and Harvey's emergence

notes: Denver is notable as one of the last teams in the NFL to not have signed a single outside free agent through March 13 — their entire offseason activity has been internal retention. The Broncos are clearly pursuing a 'run it back' strategy following their 14-win, AFC Championship Game season. Cap space entering FA was estimated at ~$33M pre-signings per Denver Post; OverTheCap lists current space at ~$18.6M. Additional cap relief of $8.19M from the Greenlaw post-June 1 cut will not be available until June 1. Ben Powers ($18.15M cap hit, no guarantees) remains a potential cut/trade candidate that could create significant additional space.
